title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXII. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old man who liv'd in Middle Row,
        He had five hens and a name for them, oh!
        Bill and Ned and Battock,
        Cut-her-foot and Pattock,
        Chuck, my lady Prattock,
        Go to thy nest and lay.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCLXII. Gaffers And Gammers.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
